objective: Multi-device workflow coordination built on M4 Relay Nodes — coordinator computes a step-to-node placement plan across healthy relay nodes, executes each workflow step on its assigned device, and falls back locally when an assigned node fails. Includes a placement planner, a placement-preview endpoint, and coordinated multi-device execution with audit trail.

placement_policies: distribute (spread model steps across capable healthy nodes, least-loaded)
local_first (local when locally capable, else route)
prefer_relay / route_if_unavailable (M4 per-step dynamic decision, unchanged)
assigned_node_routing: companion/relay/router.js executeStepWithAssignedNode (route to specific node, fallback local + RELAY_FALLBACK audit on failure)
server_wiring: applyRelayPlacement() attaches assignments + placementSummary for relay_policy=distribute on /tracks/run and /workflows/run
tests: scripts/test-multi-device-e2e.cjs (22/22 — three Local Brain instances A orchestrator + B + C relay; distributed run, then kill C -> local fallback)

all_test_results.test:relay (unit): 17/17 PASS (harness fixed: async checks now actually run)

results: Placement planner distributes model steps across two relay nodes (B gets priority_helper, C gets developer/guardrail/testing writers) and keeps tool steps local
Multi-device e2e: Lighthouse Handoff runs on A with model steps executed on B and C; relay_placement summary reports 4 relayed / 6 local across both devices
Failure + local fallback: killing node C reroutes its steps locally and records RELAY_FALLBACK audit events; workflow still completes
Existing M4 relay e2e still passes (single-step prefer_relay unaffected)

passed: Coordinator computes a placement plan across 2+ relay nodes for a workflow
Each step executes on its assigned device; tool steps stay local
Node failure mid-workflow falls back to local execution with audit trail
Placement preview endpoint reports assignments + summary
All existing tests still pass (backward compatible)
End-to-end: Lighthouse Handoff distributed across 3 devices, then degraded to 2 with fallback

scope_notes: Single orchestrator, ephemeral relay nodes — no distributed consensus or global scheduler
Placement is capability + health + least-loaded; no latency/bandwidth awareness
Tool steps are always local by design (relay nodes are model-capability targets)
Local fallback is per-step; a failed node is marked unhealthy for 60s (registry stale window)
stop_conditions_honored: Local Brain localhost-only by default (no public exposure)
Relay nodes are execution targets only (no control-plane ops)
No automatic model swapping / Model Garage
No distributed consensus / Byzantine fault tolerance claimed
Failures degrade gracefully to local execution (no data loss)
last_fresh_validation: 2026-07-11 (M5 complete + review fixes — multi-device workflow coordination)
next_milestones: M6: Trusted Relay Execution and Actual-Placement Evidence — node pairing/authentication, capability verification, allowed-network restrictions, minimal-context envelopes, planned-vs-actual placement records, remote output schema validation, explicit relay fallback reasons, one real two-device pilot, performance comparison, human-readable operator view

architectural_review_2026-07-11.scope: M5 architectural review for safety, evidence integrity, and deployment readiness

findings.title: Relay communication has no visible trust boundary
findings.description: No authentication token, signature, node certificate, request nonce, or pairing credential. Registration and heartbeat calls are unauthenticated. A rogue or accidentally registered LAN node could receive workflow context, user-derived content, and return manipulated results.
findings.fix: M6 objective #1: Node pairing and authentication

findings.title: Planned relay placement can silently become local execution
findings.description: When an assigned node is missing or unhealthy, executeStepWithAssignedNode() falls back to local execution without recording a fallback audit. Run reports placement plan assigning step to relay node, but actual execution occurred locally with no recorded reason.
findings.consequence: Planned and executed topology can diverge silently, weakening the evidence system

findings.title: local_first defaults to effectively local-only
findings.description: Every role is treated as locally capable when localCapableRoles is omitted. local_first immediately assigns locally when the role is considered locally capable.
findings.consequence: Without explicit local-capability data, relay nodes are never used for model steps
findings.fix: Document that local_first requires explicit localCapableRoles for correct behavior

findings.title: Approved evidence was written with an agent as approver
findings.description: Several new evidence records use approvedBy: locaily-agent. Blurs distinction between generated, promoted, machine-reviewed, human-reviewed, and approved for qualification.
findings.fix: Use promotionActor instead of approvedBy; reserve approvedBy for actual human approval

architectural_review_2026-07-11.recommendation: M6: Trusted Relay Execution and Actual-Placement Evidence — harden one real two-device path, prove the evidence is truthful, and measure whether distribution creates value
architectural_review_2026-07-11.assessment: Today's work passes as a major milestone. The structure is disciplined, modular, test-oriented, and aligned with the Locaily thesis. The system did not take the lazy route of bolting HTTP calls onto the workflow runner; it created actual placement, registry, protocol, routing, evidence, and fallback concepts. The next danger is scope excitement.

review_2026-07-11.scope: Post-completion review of M4 + M5 for bugs, mistakes, and unfinished items
issues_found: registry.selectForRole sorted descending (picked most-loaded node) — wrong least-loaded behavior
placement byRole double-counted a node advertising both `role` and `role:role`
no direct unit test for executeStepWithAssignedNode (M5 assigned-node routing)
relay-node-protocol.md documented M4 only, missing M5 placement/distribute/POST /relay/plan
HIDDEN: test-relay-unit.cjs async checks were fire-and-forget before process.exit — never executed (false confidence); exposed a wrong audit-field assertion (r.code vs r.error_code)
fixes_applied: companion/relay/registry.js: selectForRole now sorts ascending (a.dispatchCount - b.dispatchCount)
companion/relay/placement.js: byRole dedupes nodes per role
scripts/test-relay-unit.cjs: harness now awaits async checks; added 3 executeStepWithAssignedNode tests; corrected audit assertion to r.error_code
docs/05-integrations/relay-node-protocol.md: added M5 placement section (distribute policy, POST /relay/plan, relay_placement, assignments)
review_2026-07-11.outcome: All M4/M5 review issues resolved; no blockers remaining for M5 acceptance.
